CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str1 VARCHAR(100), str2 VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (1, 'hello', 'world');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (2, 'foo', 'bar');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (3, 'string', 'concatenation');
SELECT str1 || str2 FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT str1 || ' - ' || str2 AS combined FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT (SELECT str1 || str2 FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1) AS first_concat, (SELECT str1 || str2 FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2) AS second_concat;
SELECT a.str1 || b.str2 FROM test_strings a JOIN test_strings b ON a.id = 1 AND b.id = 2;
SELECT id, str1 || ' ' || (SELECT str2 FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3) AS combined FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT str1 || COALESCE(NULLIF(str2, ''), 'default') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT CONCAT(str1, '...', str2) AS combined FROM test_strings WHERE id < 3;
INSERT INTO another_strings (str_value) VALUES ('This is '), ('a concatenated '), ('string example');
SELECT (SELECT str_value FROM another_strings WHERE str_value LIKE 'This%') || ' with additional text.';
SELECT id, str1 || ' has length ' || CAST(LENGTH(str1) AS VARCHAR) FROM test_strings;
SELECT 'Concatenated: ' || (SELECT str1 FROM test_strings ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1) AS latest_concat;
